content: Anya sent a pricing model spreadsheet at 2:14pm showing per-seat tiers: Starter $299/mo (3 seats), Pro $799/mo (10 seats), Enterprise custom. She noted predictability aids forecasting. Sebastien hasn't replied yet.
summary: Anya proposes per-seat pricing model with three tiers

content: Per-seat pricing locks revenue by headcount; per-token scales with actual usage. Finance prefers seats (budgeting clarity). Product/engineering worry seats cap growth if customers add users but don't increase token spend.
summary: Core tension: seats = budgeting predictability vs. tokens = usage alignment

content: Sebastien said in standup: 'I want pricing that doesn't penalize power users. A seat model feels like 2010.' Lina nodded; Anya stayed quiet. Decision deferred to next Thursday meeting.
summary: Sebastien rejects per-seat; Lina aligns with usage-based thinking

content: When pricing debates arise, always document the core assumption each stakeholder is protecting: finance = cash predictability, product = customer lock-in avoidance, CEO = margin protection. Gets revisited. Write it down the same day.
summary: Protocol: surface and document assumptions in pricing disagreements

content: Acme Corp (our biggest customer, $40k ARR) would hit 12 users by Q2. Under Anya's per-seat model at $80/seat, that's $11.5k/mo. They currently use ~2.1M tokens/mo; per-token at $0.004/k tokens = $8.4k/mo. Anya's model wins here.
summary: Per-seat model favors Clude on Acme's growth trajectory

content: Anya came to my desk with her laptop. 'Look, we're at $1.2M ARR. Per-seat gets us to $1.8M by EOY if we land 3 more mid-market deals. Per-token keeps us flatter.' She was visibly stressed about guidance.
summary: Anya crunches numbers; per-seat scenario shows 50% upside vs. per-token

content: Per-token pricing risks: customers will optimize token usage (compression, caching, model selection), eroding revenue. Per-seat risks: customers stay on free tier longer or negotiate for user bundling. Both have leakage vectors.
summary: Both models have hidden erosion mechanisms; pure comparison misleading

content: Before any pricing decision at Clude: run sensitivity on top 5 customers with actual usage data. Forecast 12-month revenue under both models. Share results in one doc; note which model breaks ties. No re-running the math later.
summary: Pricing decision SOP: model top 5 customers, 12-month forecast, one source of truth

content: Lina said in Slack: 'If we're per-seat, we have to cap token usage or customers will max out and complain.' Anya replied: 'That's a support nightmare.' Sebastien said nothing. Both points are valid. Deadlock feeling real.
summary: Product constraint: per-seat + uncapped tokens = broken unit economics

content: Initech (prospect, $30k/mo budget) is deciding between us and Voyage AI. Their usage pattern is bursty (research sprints). Per-token punishes them badly on spike months; per-seat would let them absorb variance without billing shock.
summary: Per-seat favors bursty usage; per-token punishes variance

content: Sebastien asked me in 1:1: 'What's the right answer?' I said: 'Hybrid. Per-seat base + overage tokens at $0.002/k above the tier allowance. Sebastien got quiet, then: 'Write that up. Friday. One page.'
summary: Sebastien opens door to hybrid model; requests RFC by Friday

content: Hybrid per-seat + token overage model: Starter $299/mo (3 seats, 500k tokens/mo), Pro $799/mo (10 seats, 2M tokens/mo), overages $0.002/k. Balances Anya's predictability need with Sebastien's usage alignment + Lina's customer fairness concern.
summary: Hybrid pricing bridges all three stakeholder concerns

content: I realized I dislike pricing debates because they have no 'right answer'—only tradeoffs that lock in for 18 months. I respond by documenting assumptions hard and pushing for small bets (pilots, grandfathering). It delays consensus but reduces regret.
summary: Maya's approach: doc assumptions, pilot changes, accept delayed closure

content: Anya read my hybrid RFC. 'Token overages will be a support drain.' Sebastien said: 'Less than a pure per-seat hard cap. Run the Acme and Initech scenarios.' Anya agreed to model it. Finally moving.
summary: Anya accepts hybrid model; modeling begins on key accounts

content: Decision rule agreed: if hybrid model shows <5% revenue difference vs. per-seat on top 10 customers, adopt it. If overage support overhead exceeds 2 hours/week, revert to pure per-seat. Testable, reversible.
summary: Hybrid model adopted with success metrics and reversion clause

content: Pricing locked: Starter $299/mo (3 seats, 500k tokens), Pro $799/mo (10 seats, 2M tokens), Enterprise custom. Overages $0.002/k tokens. Grandfathered existing customers on old terms for 12 months. Rollout: Q1 2026.
summary: Hybrid pricing model finalized and scheduled for Q1 rollout
